meta.title: Subagent Invocation Patterns Analysis
meta.description: Comprehensive analysis of subagent usage patterns extracted from real session data

parallel.description: Tasks array mode — multiple concurrent subagents

single.description: Single task mode — one isolated subagent

chain.description: Sequential chain mode — never observed in sessions

patterns.name: PR Review Squad
patterns.description: Launches 5-8 named reviewer agents for comprehensive PR review. Each agent has a distinct persona/focus area.

tasks.task: Review PR #... for architectural concerns, design patterns, and structural integrity.
Security review of PR changes — identify vulnerabilities, auth issues, data exposure risks.
Performance review — identify N+1 queries, memory leaks, unnecessary allocations, slow paths.
Simplicity review — flag over-engineering, unnecessary abstractions, complex logic.
Agent accessibility review — ensure code is readable and maintainable by AI coding agents.
Search docs/solutions/ for relevant prior learnings and context.
Pattern analysis — identify recurring patterns, anti-patterns, and consistency issues.

patterns.name: Parallel File Reading
patterns.description: Multiple parallel tasks to read documentation or source files. Always uses claude-haiku-4-5 for cost efficiency. Tools explicitly scoped to read-only.

tasks.task: Read the pi extensions docs and summarize the key APIs and patterns.
Clone and examine repository structure, identify main entry points.

patterns.name: Lightweight Single Recon
patterns.description: Single task with haiku model for fast repository exploration and reconnaissance. Broad tool access for navigation.

example.task: List all nix files in this repository and give a brief summary of each one's purpose.

patterns.name: Named Agent Research
patterns.description: Single agent with a named persona for focused research on a specific topic. Uses default model and tools.

example.task: Understand existing patterns related to billing subscription lifecycle management in this codebase.

patterns.name: Creative / Tool-less
patterns.description: Pure LLM generation with explicitly empty tools array. No file access or execution — just text generation.

example.task: Write a single haiku poem about the joy of parallel computation.

patterns.name: Simple Parallel Test Tasks
patterns.description: Very simple parallel tasks used for testing and debugging subagent behavior. Minimal prompts, no tools, haiku model.

source_distribution.note: nix-home dominates due to iterative subagent development/testing in that repo

key_observations.observation: The `agent` parameter is used exclusively for named reviewer personas, primarily in PR review squads.
key_observations.impact: Provides role-based framing that shapes each subagent's review focus and tone.

key_observations.observation: `model: "claude-haiku-4-5"` is used for all lightweight and read-only tasks.
key_observations.impact: Cost optimization — haiku is ~10x cheaper and sufficient for file reading and simple generation.

key_observations.observation: `cwd` is set for World monorepo tasks to provide zone/service context.
key_observations.impact: Ensures subagents operate in the correct directory scope within large monorepos.

key_observations.observation: Tools are explicitly scoped — read-only for recon, empty for pure generation.
key_observations.impact: Principle of least privilege: subagents only get the capabilities they need.

key_observations.observation: No `systemPrompt` customizations were observed — all invocations use the default system prompt.
key_observations.impact: Task descriptions alone are sufficient for guiding subagent behavior in practice.

key_observations.observation: No chain mode usage was found across any sessions.
key_observations.impact: Sequential workflows are either handled in the main context or not yet adopted.

key_observations.observation: `maxConcurrency: 8` was seen once, in a PR review with 8 parallel reviewer agents.
key_observations.impact: Default concurrency is usually sufficient; explicit limits only needed for large fan-outs.

task.notes: Always present — the core instruction for every subagent.

tasks.notes: Parallel mode dominates; used for reviews, file reading, and testing.

agent.notes: Named personas used in PR reviews and focused research tasks.

model.notes: Overridden to claude-haiku-4-5 for lightweight tasks; default model used for complex work.

tools.notes: Explicitly scoped when needed; omitted to use defaults for full-capability tasks.

cwd.notes: Set for monorepo tasks requiring specific directory context.

systemPrompt.notes: Never used — task descriptions provide sufficient guidance.

chain.notes: Chain mode never observed in any session.
